name: Benin (1975–1990)
description: A plain green field with a single red five-pointed star in the upper-hoist canton.
history: Adopted on 30 November 1975 when the country was renamed from Dahomey to the People's Republic of Benin under the Marxist–Leninist government of Mathieu Kérékou, replacing the 1959 Pan-African tricolour. It served as the national flag until 1 August 1990, when the country abandoned Marxism–Leninism and restored the original 1959 design.

features.symbolism: the Marxist–Leninist orientation of the People's Republic of Benin
